I retained Angelina Ray for representation in a restraining order matter. My experience raised significant concerns about communication, case management, and billing practices.

Because of these issues, I pursued fee arbitration through the local bar association. The arbitrator ruled in my favor and awarded reimbursement of a portion of the fees I had paid.

I encourage anyone considering legal representation to carefully review their retainer agreement, monitor billing statements closely, and ensure they feel confident in the communication and strategy being provided.

I was blind-sided at mediation when NONE of my evidence was available for reference. Responses were not very timely, and office staff was rotating. I had to start recording my voicemail messages when reaching out since I was unable to get a hold of anyone after multiple attempts.  In retrospect, I have full faith I would've seen better results representing myself. Advice was questionable at times, but I was trying to trust that a professional knew how to handle the overall legal process best. She expressed unprofessional comments to me about my case during the hearing and by that time I felt like I was 100% alone fighting for my child's best interest and had an EX and what felt like his 2 lawyers to address. If she did not agree or support me as her client and she was not a right fit for my case, she should not have taken it. Outcome was not favorable in ANY aspect and after many years of my child suffering, I am finally able to address all my child has gone through with the aweful results she attained. Go with your gut instincts, and please fully research ANY legal counsel before hire. Don't let their Christan decor or "blessings" sign-offs fool you like it did me, in the end their true colors showed and it left my child hanging while they collected a paycheck. I would not recommend under ANY circumstances.
reviews.ownerResponse: I take feedback seriously, but I do not have any record of representing the individual described in this review. Due to confidentiality obligations, I cannot address specific allegations publicly. If this review was intended for my office, I encourage the reviewer to contact me directly to clarify whether there has been a misunderstanding.

reviews.ownerResponse: I think this review is a great opportunity for the firm to explain the process.  We get over a hundred calls per month for our services.  We simply cannot take each and every case and in most cases cannot talk to each person.  But we do try to get back to each potential client with a referral or a decision on your case.  Just because we don't take your case, it doesn't mean it does not have value.  It does not mean that we are only taking cases because we want to make more money.  In fact, we take many cases that we deem righteous and will not be financially beneficial.  We have to make sure we take the right case for our firm and not take too many so as to dilute our efforts.  You are free to call other lawyers.  Many others will not ever phone you back.  I think potential clients have a strong belief that they have a strong case and are disappointed when we pass.
